body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: Authenticated Chunks for the Stream Control Transmission Protocol (SCTP)
7. DTLS security over SCTP, Design Group Report
9.1  Media Header Extensions for Wireless Networks

of the things that had been identified in the l4s drafts uh as they were progressing was the need to um limit the burstiness uh or recommend limiting burstiness of uh links that support uh l4s very similarly uh it make sense for uh implementations of the nqb phb to also limit the amount of burstiness that they introduce into U the traffic stream that that um continues Downstream from them um so a new section was added to um discuss that aspect and and requirements around that um second item uh there a couple of comments related to uh the section on interoperability with non- diff serve capable domains um and specifically the comments paraphrase here um that there were three options provided one is that the Upstream domain remarks all nqb traffic to zero which was the primary recommendation in the draft um and then other options were to uh use traffic protection and only remark the offending traffic um and lastly use traffic protection and drop the offending traffic um the comments were that uh drop should be discouraged um in this uh context that um they uh um the other uh mechanisms are preferred um and uh drop was kind of draconian here where the traffic protection really is not identifying a queuing delay that's happening in the downstream domain it's um it's just kind of a preemptive traffic protection um uh and then the second part of the comment was that rather than recommending that remarking all traffic to zero um rather than than recommending that is the primary recommendation that uh traffic protection should be the primary recommendation um after further discussion um with the other authors and uh and uh Bob uh um we did agree to discourage the packet drop option but um decided to leave the other two options as equal footing rather than having one be a primary recommendation and one being an alternate so I believe that that resolves um that that pair of comments uh and then the other Technic technical comments were on traffic protection um uh there a few related to that section so one um it wasn't clear that traffic protection was intended to be implemented locally at the phb node um and then um uh kind of adding to that confusion was that uh policing at the Ingress Edge was also discussed in that section so it left um it unclear as to um um what the distinction was there and so the ask was for more clarity between um the traffic protection and traffic conditioning and uh and really spell that out better um and then uh add uh the option of reclassifying traffic without remarking as a third possible action so um uh the traffic protection section included already um class reclassification with remarking and packet drop as to possible actions and suggestion here was to uh add this third possible action and then there were a few other minor changes so I think we've tackled all of those um with some significant uh uh changes in section 5.2 um hopefully that uh makes it a lot more clear for people who picked this up for the first time uh and then the long list of nits that involved found um those were all resolved by um late in December um put a pointer to the mailing list uh post where um we discussed all those items so um that's it for the changes in draft 22 um and next slide so at this point um I think um the authors feel that uh we have addressed all the changes that uh came out of working group last call and all
